name: Adaptive Security
notes: AI-native cybersecurity startup founded by serial entrepreneurs Brian Long (CEO) and Andrew Jones. Builds platform simulating AI-powered attacks — deepfake voice/video phishing, smishing, and GenAI social engineering — to train corporate defenders. name: Pathos Consulting Group
notes: AI ethics consulting firm focused on K-12 education, founded by Evan J. Harris (President). Specializes in helping independent schools integrate AI tools responsibly — staff department automation, student AI empowerment, and ethical professional development. name: Global AI Frontier Lab
notes: Joint U.S.-South Korea AI research center at NYU, established May 2024 via MOU between NYU and Korea's IITP. Co-directed by Yann LeCun and Kyunghyun Cho. Focuses on three pillars: fundamental AI research, trustworthy/responsible AI, and AI for healthcare. Korean government investing ~$34M (45B won)

name: House Democratic Commission on AI and the Innovation Economy
notes: House Democratic caucus body launched Dec 2025 by Leader Hakeem Jeffries. Co-chaired by Rep. Ted Lieu (CA-36), Rep. Josh Gottheimer (NJ-05), and Rep. Valerie Foushee (NC-04). name: MIT Schwarzman College of Computing
notes: MIT's cross-disciplinary computing college founded in 2018 with a $1B endowment anchored by a $350M gift from Stephen Schwarzman. name: Education and Workforce Committee
notes: U.S. House committee with jurisdiction over labor, education, and workforce training policy as it intersects with AI. name: University of California, Los Angeles
notes: Major public research university and UC system flagship. Launched the AI Innovation Initiative — a campus-wide effort to build AI fluency and drive responsible AI adoption. First California university to partner with OpenAI (Sept 2024), also partnered with Google. Supported 68 AI pilot projects acro

name: International Trade Administration
notes: Bureau within U.S. Dept of Commerce responsible for trade promotion and commercial diplomacy. Leads the American AI Exports Program (per Trump July 2025 EO) to promote export of U.S. AI technology stack globally. Establishing National AI Center as gateway for U.S. AI exports. Has a dedicated AI Team

name: MIT Data to AI Lab
notes: MIT research group within the Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems (LIDS), founded in 2015 by Principal Research Scientist Kalyan Veeramachaneni. Develops large-scale AI and ML systems that work alongside humans in applications spanning healthcare, finance, education, and energy. Notable

name: Pulitzer Center
notes: Nonprofit journalism organization (Pulitzer Center on Crisis Reporting) supporting independent journalists on underreported global issues. Major AI accountability journalism hub: runs AI Accountability Network with dedicated fellowships, reporting grants, ML reporting grants, and AI Spotlight Series

name: California Department of Technology
notes: California's central IT agency (CDT), oversees state technology policy and services. Leads AI integration for state government under Gov. Newsom's executive orders (EO N-12-23). Launched 'Poppy,' a GenAI digital assistant for state employees built on large language models, with 20+ state departments

name: California Department of Human Resources
notes: CalHR manages California's state workforce (civil service rules, benefits, training). name: NYS Office of Information Technology Services
notes: New York State's central IT agency (ITS). Authored the state's 'Acceptable Use of Artificial Intelligence Technologies' policy (Jan 2024) under Gov. Hochul's direction, requiring leadership approval for AI systems, human oversight for automated decisions, and NIST AI RMF-based risk assessments. Appo

name: Kempner Institute

name: Stanford Center for AI Safety

name: High-Flyer AI
notes: High-Flyer (Zhejiang High-Flyer Asset Management) is a Chinese quantitative hedge fund founded by Liang Wenfeng. In 2023 it incubated DeepSeek as a spin-off AI lab, funding it internally. High-Flyer posted 56.6% average returns in 2025 and manages over 70 billion yuan (~$10B USD).

name: Universidad de Buenos Aires
notes: Argentina's largest public university, accounts for ~40% of national research output. IALAB published guide on responsible use of generative AI for judiciary, modeled on MI

name: Senate AI Caucus
notes: Bipartisan U.S. Senate caucus on artificial intelligence, launched March 2019 by Sens. Martin Heinrich (D-NM) and Rob Portman (R-OH). Original members included Schatz (D-HI), Gardner (R-CO), Peters (D-MI), Ernst (R-IA). After Portman's retirement (2023), Mike Rounds (R-SD) became co-chair. Heinrich

name: Stability AI
notes: Open-source generative AI company (est. 2019). Creator of Stable Diffusion. Founded by Emad Mostaque, who stepped down as CEO in March 2024 citing desire to 'fix concentration of power in AI.' name: Seoul National University

name: Trail of Bits

name: Public Interest Technology University Network

name: Oxford Martin School

name: AT&T Bell Laboratories
notes: Legendary corporate research lab (est. 1925). name: Gigafund

name: B Capital
notes: Global venture capital firm co-founded in 2015 by Eduardo Saverin (Facebook co-founder) and Raj Ganguly. Over $9B in AUM across 9 offices and 100+ employees. Invests seed through late-stage in enterprise software, AI/ML, fintech, healthcare tech, and security. Led Goodfire's $150M Series B at $1.25B

name: Mayo Clinic

name: The Hastings Center
